schemaMeta.name: Uniswap V3 Mainnet
schemaMeta.description: Core protocol entities for the Uniswap V3 subgraph on Ethereum mainnet, including pools, ticks, and swaps.

tables.name: factories
tables.description: The Uniswap V3 Factory entity tracking total volume, TVL, and pool counts.

tables.name: pools
tables.description: Liquidity pools created by the factory. Contains fees, liquidity, and token references.

tables.name: swaps
tables.description: Swap events across all pools. High-frequency data used for volume and price analysis.

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Welcome to DataBard Web3! Today we're auditing the Uniswap V3 Mainnet subgraph — 8 entities, health score 68 out of 100. Let's see how the indexers are holding up.

script.speaker: Morgan
script.text: We've got some sync issues. Indexer lag is showing up in the pools entity, and we're seeing zero-value swap entries that shouldn't be there. Protocol health is at risk.

script.speaker: Morgan
script.text: The pools entity is flagging an indexer lag check. Volume statistics haven't refreshed in 25 minutes, which is an eternity for high-frequency DeFi data.

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Pools are the heart of V3. 12,500 active pairs. If the volume data is stale, every downstream dashboard and arbitrage bot is flying blind.

script.speaker: Morgan
script.text: And look at the swaps entity. 45 million records, but we're seeing a spike in failed 'nonzero_amount' tests. Some events are being indexed with zero USD value.

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: That usually points to a price oracle failure in the subgraph mapping. If the token price isn't resolved at the block height, the swap value drops to zero.

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: On the positive side, the factory entity is perfectly in sync. TVL and pool counts are matching the contract state exactly.

script.speaker: Morgan
script.text: We should talk about Initia minting. Since this is Uniswap's core health, we need to record this state on Initia to provide a verifiable audit trail for the DAO.

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: The flow is clear: factories deploy pools, and pools generate swaps. If the factory metadata drifts, the entire subgraph structure is compromised.

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: That's the Uniswap V3 audit — health score 68. Some indexer turbulence but core factory state is solid.

script.speaker: Morgan
script.text: Priority one: debug the price oracle mapping in the swaps handler. Priority two: check the RPC provider for pools volume sync lag. Keep the chain moving.
